messages.content:
The Eiffel Tower is an iconic iron lattice tower located on the Champ de Mars in Paris, France. Named after its designer, the engineer Gustave Eiffel, the tower was constructed from 1887 to 1889 as the entrance arch to the 1889 World's Fair, which celebrated the 100th anniversary of the French Revolution. At 324 meters (1,063 feet) tall, it was the tallest man-made structure in the world until the completion of the Chrysler Building in New York City in 1930. 

The Eiffel Tower is one of the most recognizable structures in the world and a global cultural icon of France. It attracts millions of visitors each year, making it one of the most-visited paid monuments globally. The tower has three levels for visitors, with restaurants on the first and second levels and an observation deck on the third level, offering stunning views of Paris. Initially met with criticism, the Eiffel Tower is now considered a masterpiece of engineering and a symbol of artistic achievement.

The Eiffel Tower was constructed between 1887 and 1889. It was officially completed on March 31, 1889. The tower was built as the entrance arch for the 1889 World's Fair, held in Paris to commemorate the 100th anniversary of the French Revolution.
